mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending recession risk, triggering risk-off positioning that compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in housing starts and industrial production indicates a broad aggregate demand shock that reduces corporate revenue expectations, depressing equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 900 while INDPRO declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-INDPRO signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising revolving credit balances reflect improving consumer confidence and disposable income, driving short-term equity momentum as retail spending expectations lift near-term corporate earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS readings do not correlate with positive 5-day SPX returns in rolling 1-year windows across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates supply chain disruptions or sudden demand collapse, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in lower near-term corporate profitability.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive or neutral following INDPRO drops exceeding 2% in historical data.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month employment growth supports corporate revenue forecasts and reduces default risk, sustaining equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 60-day SPX returns across multiple market cycles, with directional accuracy failing to exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous federal funds rate hikes and accelerating consumer price inflation compress real yields and trigger risk-off positioning, depressing equity returns over the next month as discount rates outpace earnings growth.
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL signal does not outperform single-series baselines in predicting negative 20-day returns, with interaction term coefficients remaining statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sub-1.5% annualized GDP growth signals economic stagnation, forcing downward revisions to corporate earnings estimates and depressing equity valuations over a quarterly horizon as market participants price in lower future cash flows.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.5% does not lead to statistically significant negative 60-day SPX returns in historical samples, with forward return medians remaining above zero.
